prompt.text: Sarah's tip: You ___ wear a Halloween costume. It's fun!

items.hintDe: Ist das ein guter Tipp oder eine Warnung? Für gute Tipps nimmst du should.
items.explainDe: Ein Kostüm zu Halloween ist eine gute Idee. Wenn jemand etwas tun sollte, nimmst du should + Grundform: You should wear a costume.

prompt.text: We ___ go in there – it's dangerous.

items.hintDe: Es ist gefährlich! Sollte man das tun – oder besser nicht?
items.explainDe: Es ist gefährlich, also sollte man es nicht tun. Die Verneinung heißt shouldn't (= should not): We shouldn't go in there.

prompt.text: You ___ always tell an adult where you are going.

items.hintDe: Einem Erwachsenen Bescheid sagen – ist das ein guter Tipp?
items.explainDe: Es ist ein guter Tipp, immer einem Erwachsenen zu sagen, wo du hingehst. Darum should + Grundform: You should always tell an adult.

prompt.text: Ron is alone at the graveyard and he is scared. He asks: 'What ___ I do?'

items.hintDe: Ron fragt um Rat. In Fragen steht should ganz vorne – gleich nach dem Fragewort.
items.explainDe: Wenn du um Rat fragst, stellst du should vor das Subjekt: What should I do? – Was soll ich tun?

prompt.text: Lara is sick. Ron tells her: 'You ___ (not) eat so many sweets!'

items.hintDe: Du brauchst die Verneinung von should – aber nicht mit don't!
items.explainDe: Die Verneinung von should heißt shouldn't (= should not): You shouldn't eat so many sweets. Mit don't kannst du should nicht verneinen.

prompt.text: Edwina is late. She asks Ron: '___ I phone my parents and tell them?'

items.hintDe: Eine Frage um Rat: Stell should an den Anfang, vor das I.
items.explainDe: Bei Fragen um Rat steht should vor dem Subjekt: Should I phone my parents? Ein do brauchst du dafür nicht.

prompt.text: It's late and very dark. We ___ ___ (go) home now.

items.hintDe: Was sollten wir tun? should + Grundform – ohne to.
items.explainDe: Nach should steht immer die Grundform ohne to: We should go home. 'We should to go' ist falsch.

prompt.text: Ask your mum about your costume for Sarah's party: '___ I ___ (wear) my witch dress or my vampire costume?'

items.hintDe: Frag um Rat: zuerst should, dann I, dann die Grundform.
items.explainDe: Fragen um Rat baust du so: Should + Subjekt + Grundform. Also: Should I wear my witch dress or my vampire costume?

prompt.text: Sarah's tips for her Halloween party: The games ___ ___ (be) too wild.

items.hintDe: Zu wilde Spiele? Keine gute Idee! Du brauchst die Verneinung + Grundform.
items.explainDe: Zu wilde Spiele sollte es nicht geben – also shouldn't. Danach kommt die Grundform: The games shouldn't be too wild.

prompt.text: You ___ scare small children. It isn't funny for them.

items.hintDe: Kleine Kinder erschrecken – ist das okay? Sicher nicht!
items.explainDe: Kleine Kinder zu erschrecken ist keine gute Idee. Wenn jemand etwas nicht tun sollte, nimmst du shouldn't: You shouldn't scare small children.

prompt.text: Henry's pumpkin is so scary. The teacher tells the class: 'We ___ keep it for our Halloween party at school.'

items.hintDe: Der Kürbis ist super gruselig! Ihn zu behalten – gute Idee oder nicht?
items.explainDe: Den Kürbis zu behalten ist eine gute Idee. Vorschläge machst du mit should + Grundform: We should keep it for our Halloween party.

prompt.text: Sarah wants good music at her Halloween party, but it ___ be too noisy.

items.hintDe: Achtung, Gegensatz mit but: Soll die Musik zu laut sein? Sicher nicht!
items.explainDe: Zu laute Musik ist keine gute Idee für die Party. Was nicht sein sollte, sagst du mit shouldn't + Grundform: it shouldn't be too noisy.

prompt.text: You should to wear a costume at Halloween.

answers.text: You should wear a costume at Halloween.
You should wear a costume at Halloween

items.hintDe: Schau genau: Steht nach should ein Wort zu viel?
items.explainDe: Nach should steht die Grundform ohne to: You should wear a costume at Halloween. Das to muss weg.
items.note: Prompt contains the intentional learner error 'should to + verb' (the error under correction).

prompt.text: You don't should go out alone at night.

answers.text: You shouldn't go out alone at night.
You shouldn't go out alone at night
You should not go out alone at night.
You should not go out alone at night

items.hintDe: Die Verneinung von should baust du nicht mit don't. Wie heißt sie richtig?
items.explainDe: should verneinst du direkt: shouldn't (= should not). Ein don't brauchst du nicht: You shouldn't go out alone at night.
items.note: Prompt contains the intentional learner error 'don't should' (the error under correction).

prompt.text: Henry shoulds put a candle in the pumpkin.

answers.text: Henry should put a candle in the pumpkin.
Henry should put a candle in the pumpkin

items.hintDe: should ist bei allen Personen gleich – auch bei he, she und bei Namen.
items.explainDe: should bekommt nie ein -s: Henry should put a candle in the pumpkin. Auch bei he/she bleibt es einfach should.
items.note: Prompt contains the intentional learner error 'shoulds' (the error under correction) — 'shoulds' is not bank vocabulary, it is the target of the fix.

prompt.text: Du solltest immer mit Freunden gehen.

answers.text: You should always go with friends.
You should always go with friends
You should always go with your friends.
You should always go with your friends

items.hintDe: Ratschlag geben: should + Grundform – ganz ohne to.
items.explainDe: Mit should sagst du, was jemand tun sollte: You should always go with friends. Nach should steht die Grundform des Verbs.
items.note: SB ex. 12 sentence 5 verbatim (should filled in).

prompt.text: Lara sollte nicht so viele Süßigkeiten essen.

answers.text: Lara shouldn't eat so many sweets.
Lara shouldn't eat so many sweets
Lara should not eat so many sweets.
Lara should not eat so many sweets
Lara shouldn't have so many sweets.
Lara shouldn't have so many sweets

items.hintDe: Sollte nicht … = shouldn't + Grundform.
items.explainDe: Wenn jemand etwas nicht tun sollte, verwendest du shouldn't (= should not): Lara shouldn't eat so many sweets.
items.note: Story line "Maybe you shouldn't eat so many sweets" adapted to Lara.

prompt.text: What should I do?

items.hintDe: Frag um Rat: Was soll ich …?
items.explainDe: What should I do? heißt: Was soll ich tun? Mit should fragst du um Rat.

prompt.text: bed / should / you / to / go / don't

items.hintDe: Wer? + should + Grundform. Das Wort don't brauchst du nicht.
items.explainDe: Nach should kommt die Grundform: You should go to bed. Ein do oder don't brauchst du bei should nie.
items.note: WB ex. 5 "He should go to bed." adapted to you-form.

prompt.text: go / alone / you / shouldn't / out / don't / to

items.hintDe: Sollte nicht: shouldn't + Grundform – ohne don't und ohne to.
items.explainDe: should verneinst du mit shouldn't, nie mit don't: You shouldn't go out alone.
items.note: SB ex. 12 sentence 7 "go on your own" rephrased in-bank as "go out alone" (own is above the gate).

prompt.text: Tips for Halloween night

pairs.right: You should go to bed early.

pairs.left: I can't find my vampire teeth.
pairs.right: You should look on the table.
You shouldn't eat so many sweets.

items.hintDe: Lies erst das Problem, dann such den passenden Tipp mit should oder shouldn't.
items.explainDe: Mit should gibst du einen Tipp, mit shouldn't eine Warnung: You should go to bed early. / You shouldn't eat so many sweets.
items.note: Situation→advice pattern from WB ex. 6; vampire-teeth-on-the-table scene from WB ex. 2.

prompt.text: What should they do?

pairs.right: We should put on our costumes.
We shouldn't go in there now.

pairs.left: The picture of the witch is so scary.
pairs.right: We should keep it for Halloween.

items.hintDe: Wer spricht? Achte auf she und we – und ob should oder shouldn't passt.
items.explainDe: should = sollte, shouldn't = sollte nicht. Satz und Situation müssen zusammenpassen: Lara is sick. → She shouldn't eat more sweets.
items.note: Lara/graveyard scenes from the story; "keep it" echoes Henry's pumpkin (rephrased in-bank as picture — pumpkin/face are above the gate).

prompt.text: I should tell my parents.

items.hintDe: Stell should an den Anfang: Should + ich/du/wir + Grundform?
items.explainDe: Fragen mit should bildest du ohne do: should wandert an den Satzanfang – Should I tell my parents?
items.note: Story line "you should phone your parents and tell them" rephrased in-bank (phone is above the gate).

prompt.text: wear / should / what / I / tonight

items.hintDe: Das Fragewort steht ganz vorn, dann kommt should, dann du selbst und die Grundform.
items.explainDe: Bei Fragen mit Fragewort gilt: What + should + I + Grundform – What should I wear tonight?
items.note: Invented in the unit world (choosing a Halloween costume).

prompt.text: Lara is eating too many sweets. Now she is sick.

answers.text: She shouldn't eat so many sweets.

distractors: She should eat so many sweets.
She don't should eat so many sweets.
She shouldn't to eat so many sweets.
items.hintDe: Wie verneinst du should? Genau ein Wort ändert sich.
items.explainDe: Die Verneinung von should ist shouldn't + Grundform – ohne don't und ohne to: She shouldn't eat so many sweets.

distractorPool: She shoulds eat so many sweets.
She should to eat so many sweets.
She doesn't should eat so many sweets.
She should eats so many sweets.

items.note: Story scene; correct sentence adapts "Maybe you shouldn't eat so many sweets".

prompt.text: Edwina wants to go into the dark building next to the graveyard. Ron is scared.

answers.text: You shouldn't go in there alone.

distractors: You should go in there alone.
You don't should go in there alone.
You shoulds go in there alone.
items.hintDe: Gefährlich! Brauchst du should oder shouldn't – und welche Form ist richtig?
items.explainDe: should bekommt nie ein -s und wird mit shouldn't verneint: You shouldn't go in there alone.

distractorPool: You shouldn't to go in there alone.
You doesn't should go in there alone.
You should goes in there alone.
You don't should go in there.

items.note: Correct sentence is the story line "You shouldn't go in there alone." verbatim; haunted house rephrased in-bank as dark building.

prompt.text: Tips for trick-or-treating

groups.label: You should …
members: tell an adult where you are going

groups.label: You shouldn't …
members: eat all the sweets in one go

items.hintDe: Gute Idee → should, schlechte Idee → shouldn't.
items.explainDe: Mit should sagst du, was beim Trick-or-Treating eine gute Idee ist, mit shouldn't, was du lieber nicht tun solltest.
items.note: Sarah's trick-or-treating list (SB ex. 12) + parents' rules (WB ex. 7); 'mean tricks'/'stay out very late' rephrased in-bank.
